title: Use the framework's documented seam, not a bypass
description: Reach framework behavior through the service container, events, and middleware rather than static back doors.
body: Use when integrating with Laravel or Symfony. Do not instantiate framework services manually, reach into globals, or scatter logic that the container should inject. Actions: register and resolve services through the container (constructor injection or, in Laravel, behind facades that proxy the container), hook cross-cutting concerns via middleware/events, and keep business logic in testable services rather than controllers. Validation signals include a test that swaps a bound implementation via the container to exercise the seam in isolation. Risk boundary: bypassing the container produces untestable, tightly coupled code; security middleware (auth, CSRF) must run through the framework pipeline, never be hand-skipped.
